QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_31

question.text: Priya has an NPS Tier I corpus of Rs.12 lakh at normal retirement (age 60). What is the minimum amount she must use to purchase an annuity?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** At normal exit (age 60/superannuation), a minimum of 40% of the corpus must be used to purchase an annuity. 40% of Rs.12 lakh = Rs.4.8 lakh. **The Trap:** At premature exit, the annuity requirement is 80%, not 40%. Candidates may confuse normal and premature exit norms. **Cross-Exam Context:** Normal exit: min 40% annuity, max 60% lump sum (tax-free). Premature exit: min 80% annuity, max 20% lump sum. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Help clients plan their annuity choice well before retirement. The annuity type and ASP cannot be changed once purchased.
